questions.question_text: How many clock hours of pre-license education does Kentucky require for a sales associate?
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires 96 clock hours from a KREC-approved school, or alternatively 6 semester credit hours from an accredited college/university.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the Kentucky licensing exam on March 15. By when must they apply for licensure?
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires candidates to apply for licensure within 60 days of passing the exam. Failure to do so may require retaking the examination.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed sales associate wants to begin practicing. What must they have first?
questions.explanation: A Kentucky sales associate must be affiliated with a principal broker before they can practice. The license must be held under the principal broker.

questions.question_text: A college student completes 6 semester credit hours in real estate. Is this sufficient for Kentucky pre-license education?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, if from an accredited institution
distractors: No, only clock hours are accepted
Only if combined with 48 clock hours
Only if the college is in Kentucky
questions.explanation: Kentucky accepts either 96 clock hours from a KREC-approved school OR 6 semester credit hours from an accredited college/university as pre-license education.

questions.question_text: The Kentucky sales associate exam consists of how many total questions?
questions.explanation: The Kentucky exam has 120 questions split into two separately-scored portions: 80 general (national) questions and 40 state-specific questions.

questions.question_text: How many hours of post-license education must a new Kentucky licensee complete?
questions.explanation: New licensees must complete 48 hours of post-license education within the first 2 years of obtaining or activating their initial license: 32 mandatory + 16 elective hours.

questions.question_text: A new licensee fails to complete the 48-hour post-license requirement within 2 years. What happens?
distractors: Required to retake the exam only
questions.explanation: Failure to complete post-license education results in automatic license cancellation. This is a strict requirement with no extension provision.

questions.question_text: Of the 48 post-license hours, how many are mandatory versus elective?
questions.explanation: The 48 post-license hours include 32 mandatory hours in specific topics (Agency, Contracts, Finance, Fair Housing, etc.) and 16 elective hours in any KREC-approved topics.

questions.question_text: Can post-license education hours count toward a licensee's annual continuing education requirement?
distractors: Yes, they count as CE hours
Only the elective hours count as CE
Only the mandatory hours count as CE
questions.explanation: Post-license hours do NOT count toward annual CE requirements. These are separate obligations with different purposes and deadlines.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum post-license credit a licensee can earn in a single 24-hour period?
questions.explanation: Kentucky limits post-license education credit to a maximum of 9 hours per 24-hour period, preventing cramming of excessive hours in a single day.

questions.question_text: An investor assigns their purchase contract to another buyer for a fee, advertising the property online. Under HB 62, this is considered what?
questions.correct_answer: Real estate brokerage requiring a license
distractors: A contract law matter, not brokerage
questions.explanation: HB 62 (2023) defines advertising for sale an equitable interest in a purchase contract as real estate brokerage. Wholesalers must hold a license or engage a licensed broker.

questions.question_text: Which activity does NOT require a Kentucky real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: An owner selling their own property
distractors: Leasing property for others for a fee
Listing a property for sale for commission
questions.explanation: Property owners selling their own property do not need a license. A license is required for performing brokerage activities for others for compensation.

questions.question_text: A person manages rental properties for landlords, collecting rent and screening tenants for a fee. Do they need a license?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, property management requires a license
distractors: No, only real estate sales require a license
Only if managing more than 10 rental units
Only if managing commercial property types
questions.explanation: Leasing or renting real estate for others is a licensed activity in Kentucky. Property management for compensation requires a real estate license.

questions.question_text: Which of the following activities requires a Kentucky real estate license?
questions.correct_answer: Listing properties for sale for others
questions.explanation: Listing, selling, buying, or exchanging real estate for others for compensation requires a real estate license. Appraisals, inspections, and lending have their own separate licensing requirements.

questions.question_text: A wholesaler hires a licensed broker to advertise their equitable contract interest. Is this permitted under HB 62?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, using a licensed broker is compliant
distractors: Only if the wholesaler also has a license
questions.explanation: Under HB 62, wholesalers must either hold a license themselves or engage a licensed broker to advertise. Using a licensed broker satisfies the requirement.

questions.question_text: What type of background check must a Kentucky license applicant complete before scheduling the exam?
questions.explanation: Kentucky requires an FBI criminal background check based on fingerprints before a candidate can schedule the licensing exam. This ensures a thorough national criminal history review.

questions.question_text: When in the licensing process must the criminal background check be completed?
questions.explanation: The FBI fingerprint-based criminal background check must be submitted before the candidate can schedule their licensing examination, not after passing.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the state portion of the exam but fails the general portion. What must they do?
questions.explanation: Since Kentucky uses a split exam, a candidate who passes one portion need only retake the failed section. However, both portions must be passed within 4 months or both must be retaken.

questions.question_text: A candidate passes the general portion in January and fails the state portion. They do not retake until June. What happens?
distractors: Only the state portion must be retaken
The general portion is valid for 1 year
questions.explanation: Both exam portions must be passed within 4 months. Since more than 4 months elapsed between January and June, the passing general score expires and both portions must be retaken.

questions.question_text: After passing the exam, a candidate waits 75 days to apply for licensure. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: The exam results may have expired
distractors: A late application fee is assessed
The candidate receives a probationary license
questions.explanation: Candidates must apply for licensure within 60 days of passing the exam. At 75 days, the 60-day window has passed, potentially requiring the candidate to retake the examination.
